candidates.stmt:
I came to the UK 17 years ago, thinking I’d only be here for six months. But I fell in love with this country and built my life and family here.

As a dad of two, I worry about the same things many parents do—rising crime, whether my wife and children feel safe, and why our council tax keeps going up when services don’t get any better.

I never thought I’d stand in an election. But I’ve reached the point where I can’t just sit back and complain—I want to try and make things better for families like mine, and yours too.

On September 18, I’d be grateful for your support. If you share my concerns for your family and our community, please vote for me.

Sheree Miller is a cultural and arts project manager living in Newham. She has been a champion for the rights of EU citizens during the turmoil caused by Brexit. She was often out in the streets before and after the referendum gathering signatures to ensure EU citizens’ rights were protected when Labour was totally absent. Sheree has been active in the local community campaigning against fly-tipping and, together with Gareth and James, she has been tirelessly gathering signatures to regulate out of hours construction in the local area.

I’m a technology lecturer dedicated to helping young people build stable, fulfilling careers. As a local resident, I was moved to act after joining the campaign against cuts to our library services. While the mayor stayed silent and the cabinet made excuses, Green councillors stood up firmly for these vital community spaces.

Labour has held power in our council for fifty years, yet they’ve failed to deliver. The changes they now propose only take us backwards. It’s time for something better - a fairer, more hopeful future for Newham.

Vote Abdulazis, Alex and James for Canning Town South, and Areeq for mayor, on May 7th!

I live locally and work as a public servant for the city. Over the last year I’ve mentored students across Newham and Tower Hamlets, and seen first hand the passion young people in our borough have to reach their potential. I’m committed to building better access to opportunities and removing barriers for young Newham residents.

As your Green councillors we will demand transparency and accountability from the Council, help fix Newham’s social housing crisis, and build clean, healthy and safe streets. Vote for James Reilly, Abdul Aziz Saud, and Alex Cline - your Green candidates for Canning Town South on 7th May. Let’s make hope normal again

Better Call Saud.

If you’re struggling with the council, your housing, or getting the support you deserve, you need someone who will listen and act.

Canning Town South isn’t just where I live. It’s home. Not just the streets and buildings, but the people. This ward is a family, and when something goes wrong, you should have someone to turn to.

I’ve spoken to parents fighting for EHC plans for their children.

I’ve listened to young professionals weighed down by rising rents and council tax.

I’ve heard from local business owners being squeezed while keeping our community alive.

As a local government officer, this is what I do every day. I know how councils work, where they fail, and how to challenge them. I help people cut through bureaucracy, be heard, and get results.

So when something’s not right, Better Call Saud.

I think the problem with politics is that too many politicians don’t reflect the lived experiences of the people they represent.

But I do.

Faced homelessness and uncertainty about where you’d stay next? That was me for two years.

Did everything right, got your degree, and still couldn’t get a job? That was me too.

Finally earning a decent salary, but still feeling crushed by the cost of living? I know that feeling.

These are lived experiences, and they’re why I will always stand up for others facing the same struggles.

I’ll work for cleaner streets, safer neighbourhoods, and real support for people of all ages.

I, Ahmed Omar S Faqai, father of 4 childerns, all born and raised in Newham. I always concerned about my childern, local community, health, education, schools, environment, jobs and street crime rate in our local area. I worked on various positions, including interpreter, fund raiser and civil enforcement officer across the whole London. I am ready to listen concerns of the local residents and act as very strong voice of local residents in the elected office. We need to work together to bring positive change for our self and for our future generations.

Tim lives in Custom House, having lived in the borough for over twenty years. He is a researcher and occasional writer, now studying Law to better help others. He likes living here but feels things could be so much better and wants to see improvements to our area. He wants safer streets, better housing, and a stronger local economy for residents. He’s standing to make Custom House a fairer and safer place to live.

Dear Neighbour,

I wanted to write to introduce myself as your candidate for Plaistow South in this by-election.

I know how difficult things have been recently, and this Labour Council, supported by the Greens and Newham Independents, have done nothing to make our area safer, cleaner or more affordable for local residents or small businesses.

YOU AND PLAISTOW SOUTH DESERVE BETTER.

I will be the strong voice that stands up for all local residents and small businesses, to put more police on our streets, stop the littering and fly-tipping that blights our area and stop the wasteful spending that we are paying for.

A vote for me is a vote for REAL change, the change we have all been waiting for.

I look forward to meeting with you very soon and together we can make Plaistow South a much better place to live and work.

Rois Miah
    Councillor Candidate and Local Resident

I've lived in Newham my whole life and in East Ham South for over 30 years. I'm so honoured to be standing as a candidate in the community I love. I'm passionate about fighting for a fairer, cleaner, safer borough and will continue to do so in whatever capacity I can.

I am standing to be selected as a Green Party councillor for Forest Gate North and Maryland because I believe local government should be rooted in care, justice and evidence, and because I want to help build neighbourhoods where people can live well, sustainably and with dignity.

My professional life has been devoted to equality, inclusion and social justice. I currently work in senior leadership in education, overseeing strategy, governance and culture across large, diverse institutions. Over the past two decades I have led work on tackling exclusion, improving access to education and public services, and amplifying the voices of marginalised people, including disabled and neurodivergent people, LGBTQ+ communities, and communities facing structural racism. I worked for a London local authority for three years, supporting young people at risk or permanent exclusion from school, and I am also a qualified secondary school teacher, a trustee and a school governor, with extensive experience of accountability, scrutiny and collaborative decision-making.

Alongside my professional experience, I bring lived experience as a parent of four children, three of whom are autistic, and I have spent many years navigating education, health and support systems in London. That experience has given me a very practical understanding of how council decisions affect daily life, from SEND provision and housing stability, to transport, air quality and access to safe, inclusive public spaces.

I also bring formal training in counselling and mediation, which shapes how I approach leadership and public service. I have undertaken work focused on listening to and engaging with faith communities, particularly where values, identity and public policy come into tension. This has reinforced for me the importance of deep listening, respect, and the ability to hold difficult conversations constructively. I am committed to anti-racist practice as ongoing, reflective work, challenging structural inequ

Rachel lives in Forest Gate with her husband and three girls. A passionate advocate for Forest Gate and Newham, Rachel has particular interests in planning, sustainable transport and community involvement.  Rachel helped to set up Newham Living Streets, a local group that advocates for walking in Newham. Her previous professional experience includes working in policy, communications, local government and the public sector.

I have been campaigning for safer, cleaner streets in Newham as a Green Party candidate for Forest Gate South. I have attended council meetings and demanded that Newham Council does more to address social issues in the burrough; I've spoken to hundreds of residents on their doorsteps and the message is that there is too much crime on the streets. I've met with drug addiction charities and asked them how we can help. They asked if we could share our survey data with them so I've done that; this will mean they can target help where it's needed and improve the lives of drug addicts, reducing crime.

I am a consultant paediatrician. I have worked all accross London from Newham Hospital to Great Ormond Street Hospital. I've been involved in charitable work all over the world. What drives me is to help people that need it, whether they are suffering from physical illness, mental illness or a lack of resources.

I am standing in close partnership with my fellow Green candidates, Zahra Kheyre and Arshan Baskaran.  Together we bring all our energy, creativity and passion to help Forest Gate South and Newham to be a fantastic place for my children and yours to grow up in.

Mariadas is a qualify electrical engineer works on building services project over 40 years Even though you may not have considered voting Conservative before, by lending us your support together we can ensure Newham plastow north has councillor serving local communities needs on social housing, social care, youth education, employment, street security, street traffic control, street cleaning, prevention of anti social behaviour, prevent street crime, good neighbouring communities protection and working towards low emission clean environments. Newham labour council failure more  than 30 years, on local communities needs. Vote conservative party candidate on 5/05/2022.

I have proudly lived in Newham for the past 28 years, and this borough is truly my home. Over the years, I’ve seen our community grow, change, and overcome many challenges together.

Professionally, I work as a local driving instructor, which has allowed me to meet people from all walks of life across our neighbourhood. Every day I work with residents, young people, and families, helping them gain confidence and independence on the road.

Living and working in Newham has given me a real understanding of the issues that matter most to local people. I care deeply about our community and want to continue supporting residents and making Newham an even better place to live for everyone.

I am a Green Party candidate for Plaistow South, where I lived for 16 years. 
I work in Newham’s voluntary sector where I’ve spent decades fighting for, and achieving, positive change. 
I joined the Newham Green Party and found it full of people who are already active in their communities and with the skills and values to do things differently. 
Vote Green Party on 7th May for a fairer and greener Newham.

I am a local resident and community activist. I founded an international social enterprise that trains young people to use technology for good.

I am also co-director of Compost Newham, a leading local voluntary sector support hub, I am determined to use my experience to help young people thrive and deliver quality public services for our community.

I have been a Newham resident for 26 years, and spent 15 years teaching in Plaistow schools and colleges. I am a safe cycling campaigner and have been pushing for a safe diversion to the Greenway, which is currently closed from Upper Road.

Vote Green on May 7th for action on Newham’s housing crisis, and to make our streets clean and safe.

https://newham.greenparty.org.uk/2026-manifesto/

I am standing for a London that is cleaner, safer and offers a better quality of life for all of its residents. I promise to fight for cleaner air, more liveable streets, properly supported services for youth and education and transparency from our local democracy. Together we can make London an example of what a green city can be.

I am secondary school teacher working at St Bonaventure’s school in Newham. I have been living in the borough for 10 years including 4 years in East Village. Having taught thousands of Newham’s children and with my own son growing up here, I am passionate about ensuring Newham is a place where we can live without the constant fear of anti-social behaviour and crime, with roads and cycling which are safe and with a cleaner environment with less litter and pollution and more recycling. The local arrogant and complacent 100% Labour Council have taken residents for granted, too busy fighting amongst themselves. Last elections we came 2nd here. This time we can go one better to hold this Labour council to account. Labour's taking your vote for granted, we're listening to you!

I’m passionate about this constituency, living myself in the Royal Docks at North Woolwich.

I want to ensure our area is clean, safe and has good public services with a built - as well as a natural - environment to be proud of, that ensures it’s residents can live healthy and happy lives.

I’m passionate about the policies and values of the Green Party that will improve the lives of everyone. After all, there can be no climate, environmental and ecological justice without social, racial and economic justice too. This is why I have fully supported a lasting ceasefire in Gaza and the safe release of the hostages. That means, in parliament, I will fight to ensure not just equality for all but to go further – to ensure equity for all, improving the opportunities people have in their lives.

I am a pragmatic Green. Tough decisions need to be made to ensure that we have a planet inhabitable for future generations, with a healthy ecosystem and clean air. The way we do things may need to be a little different. Those that can afford it – the top 1% of earners – should contribute more, and carbon emitting businesses making unexpected huge profits should pay pay windfall taxes.

A Starmer’s Labour government, almost indistinguishable from the Tories - backtracking on their environmental, climate and social promises - will need robust scrutiny and a strong presence in parliament to prevent them veering ever more to the right. The Green Party will do that and more.

I want to make a better greener West Ham & Beckton, a better greener country, a better greener world.

I am a social entrepreneur, mother of two, and a passionate community advocate with deep roots in Newham. Having experienced the pandemic’s impact and the pressures on our local healthcare system, she is driven to ensure no resident is left behind.

I have represented bereaved families on national platforms, given hundreds of hours in community work, and am a tireless campaigner for better local services.

I believe a council’s primary duty is to care for its people, focusing on everyday issues, from fixing broken pavements to making sure our streets remain safe and clean, which makes a real difference to our lives.

I live in Stratford, East London and work as an IT professional alongside being a committed activist for affordable housing and accessible public transport.

People deserve a real choice in this election and the Green Party’s message is one of hope and optimism.

Climate change is one of many tough challenges we’re facing – but it’s not the only one. People are hurting – worsening inequality and the austerity agenda have been fuelling the cost of living crisis for years, and now a third of London’s children now live in poverty.

We need a new generation of political leaders who will stand up for what’s right – from investing in the future of our planet and in future generations by sharing out the wealth held at the top, to investing in peace in Gaza.

London's highest ever house prices demand an affordable housing revolution; soaring energy bills demand a clean energy revolution; and food prices, inflation, and corporate greed demand fair wages and an economy that’s centred on people.

The Green Party knows that these challenges are linked, and that Green policies will protect our planet, reverse decades of economic inequality, and invest in public services like our NHS to help people who need it most.

I’m standing in this election because the Green Party know there are solutions to these challenges, and together we can make our vision for a fairer Britain a reality.

I’m excited to have been chosen as the Green Party candidate for Stratford and Bow and look forward to hearing from residents in the community and to championing their concerns throughout the campaign.

If I am elected I will aim to reduce crime and take firm action on flytipping and antisocial behaviour in Wall End. Families feel unsafe, our streets ate dirty with more and more flytipping, our vehicles are veing vandalused, broken bottles and drug use are common near schools.

Wall End needs change and that change begins here. If elected, I pledge to collaborate closely with our local Safer Neighbourhood Police team. I will push for a consistent police presence and to estavlush temporary CCTV facilities to enhance the safety of our streets. My priority will be to set police goals that truly protect the people of Wall End.

It's time for a local councillor who listens, takes action and takes responsibility. Vote for a safer, cleaner and community led Wall End by voting for me.
